root_text.sn35.183-185:1.1: “Yo, bhikkhave, anattā, tatra vo chando pahātabbo, rāgo pahātabbo, chandarāgo pahātabbo.
root_text.sn35.183-185:1.3: Rūpā, bhikkhave, anattā; tatra vo chando pahātabbo, rāgo pahātabbo, chandarāgo pahātabbo.
root_text.sn35.183-185:1.8: dhammā anattā; tatra vo chando pahātabbo, rāgo pahātabbo, chandarāgo pahātabbo.
root_text.sn35.183-185:1.9: Yo, bhikkhave, anattā tatra vo chando pahātabbo, rāgo pahātabbo, chandarāgo pahātabbo”ti.

translation_text.sn35.183-185:0.3: Desire, Etc. for the Not-Self Exterior
translation_text.sn35.183-185:1.1: “Mendicants, you should give up desire … greed … desire and greed for what is not-self.
translation_text.sn35.183-185:1.3: Sights, sounds, smells, tastes, touches, and ideas are not-self …”
